About Me

Results-driven.
People-first.

I am a results-driven professional with over 15 years of experience in retail banking operations in the United Arab Emirates, specialising in processing administration, credit compliance, and collections recovery.

Recognised for exceptional leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills, I was instrumental in full department day-to-day operations at Emirates NBD — growing teams, building reporting systems, and reducing errors by up to 50% through strict process improvements.

Currently completing a Bachelor of Arts in International Business Administration with a focus on Digital Marketing at SRH University of Applied Sciences, Berlin — combining proven operational expertise with emerging skills in digital strategy, influencer marketing, and go-to-market planning.

I thrive in environments that reward analytical thinking, cross-functional collaboration, and a proactive, ownership-driven mindset.

Work Experience

Career Timeline

December 2011 – July 2023  ·  11+ Years
Senior Processing Administrator
Emirates NBD Bank — Retail Banking Sales, Personal Loans · U.A.E.

YoY

Top
Yearly appraisal grades — consistently above expectations
Key Achievements
  • Award Recognised as Best Processor of the Year for 5 consecutive years, reflecting consistently top-ranked performance across all processing KPIs, service delivery benchmarks, and compliance standards.
  • Impact Designed and implemented a management reporting system using advanced Excel — including formulas and pivot tables — delivering month-on-month and year-on-year performance comparisons that enabled data-driven target management and resource planning for senior leadership.
  • Impact Reduced application processing errors by approximately 30–50% by introducing a rigorous first-level checking protocol aligned with bank policies and compliance procedures, significantly decreasing discrepancies before files reached the Retail Credit Centre (RCC) and final compliance team.
  • People Led full-cycle recruitment, onboarding, and training of new support team members — growing the team substantially and directly contributing to improved department-wide performance against expected targets, as recognised by senior management.
  • Strategy Served as department champion for non-financial targets including Customer Service Levels and compliance; engaged in daily business-outcome discussions with VP-level leadership and supported department strategy reviews.
Responsibilities
  • Monitored and enforced compliance and control measures across all processing activities, protecting the bank's regulatory standing and internal governance requirements.
  • Identified and closed critical process and system gaps; proposed procedural improvements based on periodic reviews that enhanced turnaround time, staffing efficiency, and service delivery quality.
  • Delivered training to new hires across systems, work processes, bank policies, and task handling; developed team competencies to increase flexibility and performance within the unit.
  • Supervised and guided team activities to achieve unit objectives; maintained strong relationships with internal stakeholders, external clients, and VP-level management.
  • Prepared and disseminated management information reports ensuring timely, accurate data for leadership decision-making.
September 2007 – December 2011  ·  4+ Years
Recovery Executive
Emirates NBD Bank — Retail Collection Department · U.A.E.
~50%+
Reduction in customers with 3+ overdue installments
100%
Recovery targets met; exceeded in multiple months
Top
Yearly appraisal grades throughout tenure
Intl.
Remote settlements with relocated overseas customers
Key Achievements
  • Target Consistently met all assigned monthly recovery targets and exceeded expectations in multiple months, earning top yearly appraisal grades throughout the role tenure.
  • Impact Reduced the volume of customers with 3 or more overdue installments by approximately 50%+ through structured, continuous follow-up — directly strengthening the bank's retail portfolio health and reducing non-performing loan exposure.
  • Solutions Delivered tailored financial solutions to over-indebted customers facing salary deductions exceeding 50%, including personal loan rescheduling proposals that reduced monthly repayment burdens, improved repayment rates, and increased customer retention.
  • Solutions Negotiated and structured final settlement proposals for customers with complex debt situations, securing management approval for smooth debt clearance and exit — balancing bank interests with customer welfare and sustainable resolution.
  • Reporting Built and maintained weekly Excel-based performance reports tracking individual and team recovery targets, case closures, and outstanding challenges — presented to management to drive strategy realignment and resource decisions.
  • Tracing Conducted skip tracing activities for absconded customers — including those who relocated abroad, changed employment, or became unreachable — using email outreach and data cross-referencing to locate and successfully negotiate remote final settlements.
Responsibilities
  • Conducted in-depth analysis of defaulter cases, gathering required documentation and initiating recovery actions in line with bank procedures and compliance requirements.
  • Collaborated cross-departmentally with related bank teams to gain full case context and align on solutions for complex, long-standing debt situations.
  • Maintained professional and empathetic customer relationships throughout the recovery process, projecting the bank's values while working towards mutually viable financial outcomes.
  • Raised operational challenges and proposed actionable solutions in regular management discussions, contributing to continuous improvement of collection department processes.

Selected Projects

October 2025 – January 2026
Go-To-Market Strategy Development
Liquitekt · Practice-Based Collaboration
Task
Develop a GTM strategy for a financial consultancy to increase lead generation and market visibility in the DACH region.
Action
Conducted in-depth competitor landscape analysis and comparative benchmarking; identified high-impact networking opportunities by mapping startup events across Berlin and Northern Germany.
Result

Market Research Competitive Analysis GTM Strategy DACH Region
July 2025 – August 2025
Influencer Marketing Strategy
Zalando × SRH University · Multinational Team Project
Task
Enhance Zalando gift card engagement and sales across 9 European markets (DE, AT, CH, IT, NL, ES, FR, PL, BE) through micro-influencer strategy.
Action
Identified and evaluated 20+ micro-influencers per market using defined KPIs — engagement rate, audience fit, content relevance — via ChatGPT, Modash, Notion, and social media analytics.
Result
Contributed to a data-driven influencer strategy delivering localised insights and influencer recommendations supporting cross-market campaign planning and engagement growth.
